picks.angle: In SEA, the real AI opportunity isn't just fancy chatbots. It's tackling messy workflows and fraud with practical agents.
picks.excerpt: Southeast Asia's practical AI agent opening lies in solving messy workflows and reducing fraud.
picks.summary:
Southeast Asia’s AI story is often told as a race to build models or launch chatbots. The more immediate opening may be smaller and more practical: software agents that reduce fraud losses, clear operational backlogs, and automate repetitive casework inside large local industries. That matters in a region where the digital economy reached US$263 billion […] 
 The post  Southeast Asia’s AI agent opening is in messy workflows  appeared first on  e27 .
picks.title: Southeast Asia’s AI agent opening is in messy workflows
picks.why_matters: For Singapore and SEA, the immediate AI opportunity is solving messy, practical problems like fraud. This shows a direct path to tangible ROI for local businesses struggling with inefficiency.

picks.angle: We thought AI would replicate a person. Now, agentic AI suggests it can replicate a team. This changes everything for how we structure work.
picks.excerpt: Can AI agents move beyond replicating individuals to replicate entire teams?
picks.summary:
The first wave of large language models won attention by replicating personal work. They wrote emails, summarised documents, generated images and videos, drafted presentations, and produced usable code. That alone was enough to reshape how many people think about productivity. But the more consequential shift is now underway. AI is beginning to move beyond individual […] 
 The post  The rise of agentic work: Can AI replicate a team, not just a person?  appeared first on  e27 .
picks.title: The rise of agentic work: Can AI replicate a team, not just a person?
picks.why_matters: The potential for AI agents to replicate entire teams challenges traditional organizational structures in Singapore. This forces leaders to rethink team roles and resource allocation.

picks.why_matters: An open-source project's anti-AI contribution policy sparks debate on AI's role in collaboration and IP, crucial for Singapore's tech community. This raises questions about future tech policy and ethics.

picks.angle: Many assume AI-generated work is undetectable. We need to rethink how we assess authenticity and collaboration in teams using AI.
picks.excerpt: It's a misconception that AI-assisted work cannot be detected.
picks.summary: It's a common misconception that we can't tell who is using LLM and who is not. I'm sure we didn't catch 100% of LLM-assisted PRs over the past few months, but the kind of mistakes humans make are fundamentally different than LLM hallucinations, making them easy to spot. Furthermore, people who come from the world of agentic coding have a certain  digital smell  that is not obvious to them but is obvious to those who abstain. It's like when a smoker walks into the room, everybody who doesn't smo
picks.title: Quoting Andrew Kelley
picks.why_matters: Detecting AI-assisted work impacts performance reviews, intellectual property, and fair collaboration within Singaporean teams. This directly addresses authenticity and trust concerns in the workplace.

picks.angle: AI isn't just about offense for bad actors; it's our best defense. OpenAI's cyber plan is a blueprint.
picks.excerpt: OpenAI detailed a five-part action plan for strengthening cybersecurity using AI.
picks.summary: OpenAI outlines a five-part action plan for strengthening cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age, focused on democratizing AI-powered cyber defense and protecting critical systems.
picks.title: Cybersecurity in the Intelligence Age
picks.why_matters: Singaporean businesses are increasingly targets of cyber attacks, and AI offers potent defense. This action plan gives local leaders a framework to consider AI's role in their cyber strategy.

picks.angle: We're all pushing for faster AI, but model evaluation is slowing us down. This is the new reality.
picks.excerpt: AI model evaluations are emerging as a significant new compute bottleneck.
picks.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
picks.why_matters: Effective AI adoption means knowing its limitations, and evaluation bottlenecks affect deployment speed. This impacts how quickly SEA teams can actually get new AI tools into production.

picks.angle: The 'all-in-one employee' job post that went viral? AI agents might be making those demands obsolete. We need to rethink roles.
picks.excerpt: AI agents may signal the end of the 'all-in-one' employee role.
picks.summary:
Around a week before I started writing this, I saw a job post on social media that was everywhere. It got a lot of attention, so I clicked to see why. The requirements were: Minimum bachelor’s degree. More than 15 years of work experience. Under 25 years old. There were plenty of other detailed requirements, […] 
 The post  AI Agents and the end of the all-in-one employee  appeared first on  e27 .
picks.title: AI Agents and the end of the all-in-one employee
picks.why_matters: The rise of AI agents means we must redefine job roles and skills in Singaporean workplaces. The 'all-in-one employee' might be obsolete, impacting hiring and development strategies.

picks.angle: My tech stack changes constantly. AI agents are making it easier for even small teams to build useful tools.
picks.excerpt: My personal tool stack for building is evolving with new, easier AI options.
picks.title: Building gets easier
picks.why_matters: The ease of building with new AI tools directly impacts a team's ability to innovate without huge dev budgets. This is critical for startups and SMEs in Singapore looking to quickly deploy solutions.

picks.angle: Johor is getting another huge data center investment. This isn't just real estate; it's the backbone for our region's AI future.
picks.excerpt: AirTrunk is investing MYR12 billion in new data centers in Johor.
picks.summary:
AirTrunk will invest MYR12 billion in Johor data centres. JHB3 and JHB4 will lift AirTrunk’s Malaysia commitment to MYR27 billion. Australian company AirTrunk plans to invest MYR12 billion, or about US$3 billion, to develop two new data centres in Johor, Malaysia. JHB3 and JHB4 will be located in Iskandar Puteri, Johor, near JHB1 and JHB2, […] 
 The post  AirTrunk to invest MYR12 billion in Johor data centres  appeared first on  TechWire Asia .
picks.title: AirTrunk to invest MYR12 billion in Johor data centres
picks.why_matters: Massive data center investments in Johor signal growing compute demand, directly impacting Singapore's tech ecosystem. This ensures the necessary infrastructure for future AI growth in the region.

all_items.summary:
Codex CLI 0.128.0 adds /goal   
The latest version of OpenAI's Codex CLI coding agent adds their own version of the  Ralph loop : you can now set a  /goal  and Codex will keep on looping until it evaluates that the goal has been completed... or the configured token budget has been exhausted. 
 It looks like the feature is mainly implemented though the  goals/continuation.md  and  goals/budget_limit.md  prompts, which are automatically injected at the end of a turn.

       Via  @fcoury
all_items.title: Codex CLI 0.128.0 adds /goal

all_items.summary:
Our evaluation of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 cyber capabilities   
The UK's AI Security Institute  previously evaluated Claude Mythos : now they've evaluated GPT-5.5 for finding security vulnerability and found it to be comparable to Mythos, but unlike Mythos it's generally available right now.


     Tags:  ai ,  openai ,  generative-ai ,  llms ,  anthropic ,  claude ,  ai-security-research ,  gpt
all_items.title: Our evaluation of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 cyber capabilities

We need RSS for sharing abundant vibe-coded apps   
Matt Webb: 
 
 I would love an RSS web feed for all those various tools and apps pages, each item with an “Install” button. (But install to where?) 
 The lesson here is that when vibe-coding accelerates app development, apps become more personal, more situated, and more frequent. Shipping a tool or a micro-app is less like launching a website and more like posting on a blog.
